entities.name: grip
entities.description: A method used to hold a badminton racket.

entities.name: forehand grip
entities.description: The most common grip in badminton, also known as the V-grip or handshake grip.

entities.name: backhand grip
entities.description: Used for shots on the backhand side, also known as the thumb grip or bevel grip.

entities.name: panhandle grip
entities.description: A variation used for net kills and some smashes.

entities.name: thumb grip
entities.description: A variation used for backhand shots, where the thumb is placed on the wider surface of the handle for more power.

entities.name: bevel grip
entities.description: A variation used for deceptive shots, where the racket is held at an angle to disguise the direction of the shot.

entities.name: racket
entities.description: The implement used in badminton.

entities.name: Footwork
entities.description: The foundation of badminton movement, essential for reaching the shuttle quickly and efficiently.

entities.name: Split step
entities.description: A small hop performed as the opponent hits the shuttle, allowing quick reaction in any direction.

entities.name: Chassé
entities.description: Lateral movement used to move along the baseline or cover the side of the court.

entities.name: Lunge
entities.description: Forward movement used to reach the shuttle when it is hit short, allowing for net shots, drops, and some smashes.

entities.name: Recovery steps
entities.description: Used to return to the center of the court after hitting a shot, ensuring quick and efficient movement.

entities.name: Six corners footwork pattern
entities.description: System for covering all six corners of the court using split steps, chassés, lunges, and recovery steps.

entities.name: clear
entities.description: A high, deep shot in badminton that sends the shuttle to the back of the opponent's court.

entities.name: defensive clear
entities.description: A high, deep shot used for defensive purposes, giving the player time to recover.

entities.name: attacking clear
entities.description: A flatter trajectory shot used to push the opponent back and create opportunities for attack.

entities.name: overhead clear
entities.description: The most common type of clear, hit above the head with a full arm swing.

entities.name: underhand clear
entities.description: A shot hit from below the waist, typically used at the net to send the shuttle to the back of the opponent's court.

entities.name: clear strategy
entities.description: The overall approach to using clears in badminton to control pace and create opportunities for attack.

entities.name: Smash
entities.description: The most powerful and aggressive shot in badminton.

entities.name: Full Smash
entities.description: A variation of the smash that involves turning sideways, raising the racket above the head, and hitting the shuttle with a full arm swing.

entities.name: Jump Smash
entities.description: A variation where the player jumps into the air to hit the shuttle, creating a steeper downward angle.

entities.name: Stick Smash
entities.description: A variation that uses less power but more placement, hit with a shorter swing and less arm speed.

entities.name: Deceptive Smash
entities.description: A variation where the player disguises the smash as a different shot to catch the opponent off guard.

entities.name: drop shot
entities.description: A soft, delicate shot that sends the shuttle just over the net.

entities.name: fast drop
entities.description: A variation of the drop shot hit with more speed.

entities.name: slow drop
entities.description: A variation of the drop shot hit with less speed, causing the shuttle to float.

entities.name: straight drop
entities.description: A drop shot that travels straight down the sideline.

entities.name: cross-court drop
entities.description: A drop shot that travels diagonally across the court.

entities.name: deceptive drop
entities.description: A drop shot disguised as a different shot to catch the opponent off guard.

entities.name: Net play
entities.description: Playing shots near the net in badminton.

entities.name: Net shot
entities.description: A soft shot that sends the shuttle just over the net, landing near the front of the opponent's court.

entities.name: Net kill
entities.description: An aggressive shot hit when the shuttle is above the net, used to win points and create opportunities for attack.

entities.name: Cross-court net shot
entities.description: A variation that sends the shuttle diagonally across the court, landing near the front corner of the opponent's court.

entities.name: Tumbling net shot
entities.description: A variation that causes the shuttle to tumble and spin as it crosses the net.

entities.name: Drive
entities.description: A flat, fast shot that travels horizontally over the net.

entities.name: Flat Drive
entities.description: A drive hit with a flat trajectory, sending the shuttle horizontally over the net.

entities.name: Push Drive
entities.description: A variation of the drive hit with less speed, causing the shuttle to land closer to the net.

entities.name: Block Drive
entities.description: A defensive drive hit when the opponent hits a smash.

entities.name: Serve
entities.description: The shot that starts every rally in badminton.

entities.name: Low Serve
entities.description: A serve used in doubles where the shuttle is hit just over the net and lands near the short service line.

entities.name: High Serve
entities.description: A serve used in singles where the shuttle is hit high and deep, landing near the back boundary line.

entities.name: Flick Serve
entities.description: A deceptive serve used in doubles to catch the opponent off guard with a flick of the wrist.

entities.name: Drive Serve
entities.description: An aggressive serve hit flat and fast, landing in the middle or back of the opponent's court.

entities.name: Doubles badminton
entities.description: A fast-paced and exciting format of badminton played by two teams.

entities.name: Front-and-back formation
entities.description: An attacking formation where one player is at the front near the net, responsible for net shots, kills, and intercepting weak returns, while the other player is at the back, responsible for clears, smashes, and drops.

entities.name: Side-by-side formation
entities.description: A defensive formation where both players stand side by side, each covering half of the court, most effective against smashes and fast shots.

entities.name: Rotation between formations
entities.description: Key strategy in doubles where teams switch from side-by-side to front-and-back formation based on rally control.

entities.name: Service strategy
entities.description: Using different serves to keep the opponent guessing and create opportunities for attack, including low serve, flick serve, and drive serve.

entities.name: Communication and coordination
entities.description: Important aspect of doubles strategy involving partners communicating about shot selection, especially when the shuttle is hit down the middle.

entities.name: Deception and variation
entities.description: Using speed, direction, and placement variations to keep opponents guessing and control court with formations and rotation.
